original: Anastasius II succeeds Gelasius I as Catholic Pope
Theodore I begins his reign as Catholic Pope, rules till 649
Rabbi Nathan ben Yehiel of Rome completes Talmudic dictionary
River Thames in London freezes over
Battle at Auneau: Henri de Guise wins
Earl Mauritius ceases siege of De Bosch due to strict monarchy
John Ford's "Lover's Melancholy" premieres in London
Battle of Tuttlingen: Beiers army under Gen Mercy beats France
1st Lutheran pastor ordained in America, Justus Falckner at Philadelphia
London's Thames River freezes over
John Carteret resigns as British Secretary of State for the Northern Department
Continental Congress approves resolution barring blacks from army
Weber's opera "Das Waldmadchen" premieres in Freiburg

South Carolina passes Ordinance of Nullification, declaring the tariffs of 1828 and 1832 unconstitutional in and unenforceable in South Carolina, precipitating the Nullification Crisis which presaged the American Civil War
Texas Rangers, mounted police force authorized by Texas Provisional Government
Liège-Visé-Maastricht railway opens
Battle of Chattanooga, Columbia & Lookout Mt begins in Tennessee
American Woman's Suffrage Association forms (Cleveland)
American inventor Joseph Glidden patents barbed wire
English author Anna Sewell sells her manuscript "Black Beauty" to Norwich publisher for £40, the novel is published soon after
Victorien Sardou's "La Tosca" premieres in Paris
1st US absentee voting law enacted by Vermont
Canadian Intercollegiate Rugby Football Union forms in Kingston
The International Conference of Rome for the Social Defense Against Anarchists opens.
Clyde Coleman of NYC patents automobile electric starter
George Ade's "County Chairman" premieres in NYC
Moshav Hertzlia forms in Israel
Conflict in the Balkans grows into an acute international crisis with major powers supporting either Austria or Serbia
Dutch Overseas Trustmaatschappij (NOT) forms
Serbian leader flees to Albania

Mexican and US representatives sign a protocol at Atlantic City, under which Pershing's troops will withdraw and each nation's army will guard the border. President Carranza of Mexico will refuse to accept it
Nine police officers and one civilian are killed when a bomb explodes at the Milwaukee, Wisconsin police headquarters building.
Béla Can forms Hungarian Communist Party
Newspaper cartoon "Gasoline Alley" first appears as a weekly feature on the Chicago Tribune's Sunday Rectangle page
Italian parliament givesBenito Mussolinidictatorial powers "for 1 year"
Radio Belgium's 1st transmission
General Feng Yu-Hsiang, a warlord from the north of China, turns on his allies and seizes Peking
1st radio-broadcast of Dutch KRO (Catholic Radio Broadcast)
Eugene O'NeillTheater (Coronet, Forrest) opens at 230 W 49th St NYC
KVI-AM in Seattle WA begins radio transmissions
Sri Aurobindo retires to lead life of seclusion, hands responsibility for followers to Mirra Alfassa (founding of Sri Aurobindo ashram)
Ruth Nichols sets women's transcontinental air flight record (Mineola, NY to California), in a Lockheed-Vega
In Washington, D.C., the FBI Scientific Crime Detection Laboratory (better known as the FBI Crime Lab) officially opens.
SN Behrman's "Rain from Heaven" premieres in NYC
The Senegalese Socialist Party holds its second congress.
German pacifist Carl von Ossietzky is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for uncovering German re-armament
National Semi-Pro Basketball Congress authorizes yellow basketball
"Life Certificates" issued to some Jews of Vilna, the rest are exterminated
Indian infantry attacks German tanks at Sidi Omar
US troops land on Suriname to protect bauxite mine
French collaborator Marquis de Brinon establishes "African Falanx"
German Fieldmarshal Erich von Manstein arrives in Starobelsk, Ukraine
US bombers based on Saipan begin 1st attack on Tokyo
House of Representatives Committee on Un-American Activities finds "Hollywood Ten" in contempt for refusing to reveal whether they are communists
"Bicycle Thieves", Italian film directed by Vittorio De Sica, starring himself and Cesare Zavattini, is released (Honorary Academy Award 1950)
WAVE TV channel 3 in Louisville, Kentucky (NBC) begins broadcasting
British steel & iron industry nationalised
Syracuse Nationals beat Anderson Packers, 125-123 in 5 OTs
UN troops begin an assault intending to end the Korean War by Christmas
US infantry division conquers Chonju Korea
British auto manufacturers Austin and Morris Motors merge
Air Force One, 1st US Presidential airplane, christened
France sends 20,000 soldiers to Algeria
1st test flight of Fokker's F-27 Friendship
Glenn Davis leads an American trifecta in the men's 400m hurdles at the Melbourne Olympics; hurdles Olympic record equalling 50.1s to beat teammates Eddie Southern and Joshua Culbreath
Mali becomes an autonomous state within French Community
Mechanisation of Thought Processes, considered the first international symposium about artificial intelligence, begins in Teddington, England
Last of Dutch military leave New Guinea
30th Heisman Trophy Award: John Huarte, Notre Dame (QB)
British rock band "The Who" begins a 22-week Tuesday night residency at the influential Marquee Club in London, England
Cards' 3rd baseman Ken Boyer is voted NL MVP
Rebellion ends in Zaire
1st TV station in Congo, Kinshasa (Zaire)
400 die of respiratory failure and heart attacks in New York City smog, the smoggiest day in the city's history
A Bulgarian plane with 82 people on board crashes near Bratislava, Slovakia.
Apollo 12 returns to Earth
US Army Lt William L Calley charged with massacre of over 100 civilians in My Lai Vietnam in March 1968, ordered to stand trial by court martial
West German President Heinemann visits The Netherlands
36th Heisman Trophy Award: Jim Plunkett, Stanford (QB)
A British Army bomb-disposal specialist is killed by a bomb in Lurgan, County Armagh
A woman is killed after members of the Irish Republican Army carry out an attack on British soldiers in Strabane, County Tyrone
American "Dan Cooper" hijacks a plane, extorts $200,000 in ransom, jumps out of the plane over Washington State and is never seen again
Braves catcher-infielder Earl Williams, wins NL Rookie of Year
Prison rebellion at Rahway State Prison, New Jersey
USSR performs underground nuclear test
2 truck hijackers freed (3/3/1974)
Most complete early human skeleton (Lucy, Australopithecus) discovered by Donald Johanson, Maurice Taieb, Yves Coppens and Tim White in the Middle Awash of Ethiopia's Afar Depression
NBA Atlanta Hawks end a 28 game road losing streak
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Miami Dolphins quarterback Bob Griese passes for 6 touchdowns in a 55-14 win over Cardinals in St. Louis
Kings' Charley Simmer fails on 8th penalty shot against Islanders
1st air-launched cruise missile tested
PLO exchanges 6 Israeli prisoners for 4,500 Palestinians & Lebanese
Test Cricket debut of Richie Richardson for the West Indies vs India in Bombay
Egyptian commandos storm captured Boeing in Malta, 60 killed
NBC miniseries "Mussolini: The Untold Story" starring George C. Scott premieres in the US
Cards reliever Todd Worrell wins NL Rookie of Year
Wrestler John Tatum charged with possession of marijuana
"Make It Last Forever" debut album by Keith Sweat is released (Billboard Song of the Year 1988)
Concert performance of Heitor Villa-Lobos' folk operetta "Magdalena" debuts at Alice Tully Hall in NYC's Lincoln Center
Communist Party resigns in Czechoslovakia
Elias Hrawi elected president of Lebanon
1st international flight from Long Island's MacArthur Airport (to Mexico)
After going 12-0, Washington Redskins lose to Dallas 24-21
Rachmon Nabijev elected president of Tajikistan
US 75th manned space mission "STS 44" Atlantis 10 launched
Yugoslavian tennis starMonica Selesbeats Martina Navratilova 6–4, 3–6, 7–5, 6–0 to win her 2nd straight WTA Tour Championship at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Boeing 734 crashes into mountain in China, kills 141
11-year-old Robert Thompson and Jon Venables are convicted of the murder of English 2-year-old James Bulger
Brady bill passes establishing 5-day waiting period for US handgun sales
End of world, according to Ukrainian sect White Brotherhood
Ireland votes to end 58-year-old amendment on divorce (50.28% to 49.72%)
Mashonaland defeat Matabeleland to win the Logan Cricket Cup
Mohammad Wasim scores 109 on Test Cricket debut, Pakistan v NZ Lahore
Israeli rapist Benny Sela escapes from police custody while being transferred to a court hearing.
Ernest Bai Koroma is re-elected President of Sierra Leone
Gangnam Style becomes the most viewed Youtube video surpassing 808 million views
The continued NHL lockout results in all games to December 14 being cancelled
"Take Your Time" single is released by Sam Hunt (Billboard Song of the Year 2015)
Chicago police officer Jason Van Dyke is charged with first-degree murder of 17-year-old African American Laquan McDonald in 2014
Tunisia declares state of emergency after an attack on a bus in Tunis carrying presidential guards kills at least 12
Turkey shots down a Russian fighter jet after claiming it had flown into Turkish airspace
Venezuelan opposition leader Luis Díaz is shot and killed days before the country's general election
International research team publishes discovery of 1,500 new viruses found in invertebrates
18 women accuse Jean-Claude Arnault with ties to the Nobel Prize Committee of sexual assault and harassment in Swedish Dagens Nyheter newspaper
Militant gunmen attack a Sufi mosque in Bir al-Abed on the Sinai Peninsula, Egypt, killing 305 people, government retaliates bombing the area

Copa Libertadores Final 2nd-leg between Buenos Aires rivals River Plate & Boca Juniors is postponed after Boca bus attacked by River fans; players suffer cuts & affects of police tear gas; River wins, 5-3 on aggregate when 2nd-leg played in Madrid, Spain (9 Dec)
Cruise boat sinks on Lake Victoria near Kampala, Uganda, killing at least 29
ICC Women's Cricket T20 World Cup, Antigua: Ashleigh Gardner with 33 from 26 balls and 3 for 22 leads Australia (106/2) to 4th T20 WC; beat England (105)
Taiwanese voters vote against referendums to legalise same-sex marriage
Data leaked from Chinese high-security Muslim Uighur security camps, housing 1 million people, show systematic brainwashing in western Xinjiang region
Hong Kong pro-democracy candidates overwhelmingly win district council elections in strong rebuke to Hong Kong leaders
New Zealand cricket batsman BJ Watling scores a marathon 205 off 473 balls to lead the home side to an innings and 65 run 1st Test victory over England at Mount Maunganui
COVID-19 surge in the US gathers further pace with death toll of 2,200 highest since May and new cases averaging 175,000 a day
Scotland's parliament votes to become the first country to make period products free
US General Services Administration officially beginsJoe Biden's transition, declaring him the "apparent winner" although Donald Trump vows to continue challenging the result
At least 27 migrants drown after their boat capsizes in the English Channel trying to reach the UK
Sweden's first female Prime Minister Magdalena Andersson resigns after 12 hours in the job after her coalition government falls apart
Three men found guilty by a jury of felony murder of black runner Ahmaud Arbery, with Travis McMichael also convicted of malice murder in Brunswick, Georgia
Anwar Ibrahim is sworn in Prime Minister of Malaysia appointed by King Al-Sultan Abdullah at the head of a coalition government
British born Flossie aged almost 27 crowned the world's oldest cat by the Guinness Book of Records
100 Nigerians abducted for ransom and one killed by armed bandits on motorcycles in northern Zamfara state, amid increasing kidnappings in the region
A23a, world's biggest iceberg at 4,000 sq km (1,500 sq miles), on the move again after being grounded in the Weddell Sea for more than 30 years
Four-day ceasefire begins in Gaza with Hamas releasing 24 hostages and Israel 39 Palestinian prisoners, allowing more urgently needed humanitarian aid into the territory
$300 billion climate deal agreed at COP29 in Baku, Azerbaijan, to help poorer countries deal with the effects of climate change, amid criticism it isn't enough
A ceasefire is agreed to end sectarian violence in north-western Pakistan, after more than 80 are killed and 150 injured in attacks between Shia and Sunni Muslims
NFL Philadelphia Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets new franchise game record with 255 yards rushing in 37-20 victory over Los Angeles Rams at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California
Theodosius Imakes his adventus, or first formal entry, into Constantinople
Battle of the Indus:Genghis Khan's Mongol force defeats Shah
Battle of Solway Moss: English beat Scottish KingJames V
Dutch explorerAbel Tasmanis the first European to discover Van Diemen's Land (Tasmania)
England's most influential general LordJohn Churchillmeets and pledges allegiance to William of Orange (laterWilliam III)
NaturalistsAlexander von HumboldtandAimé Bonplanddepart Caracas for Cuba to meet fellow botanist John Fraser, during their five-year expedition of Spanish America
English naturalistCharles Darwinpublishes "On the Origin of Species" radically changing the view of evolution and laying the foundation for evolutionary biology
M Levy publishesGustave Flaubert's historical novel "Salammbô"
PhotographerAlfred Stieglitzopens the Little Galleries of the Photo-Secession in Manhattan, New York
Benito Mussolinileaves Italy's Socialist Party
1st Dutch airliner,Fokker F.VIIlands in Batavia (Java) after flying from Amsterdam

Newark-Jersey City Viaduct,  a 3.5 mile long highway bridge-causeway over the Passaic and Hackensack rivers, and the New Jersey Meadowlands opens; later named the GeneralCasimir PulaskiSkyway, in honor of the Polish military leader who fought in the US Revolutionary War
Fred Astaire's first film, "Dancing Lady," is released
Noël Coward's anthology "Tonight at 8:30" premieres in NYC, runs for 118 performances
Clifford Odets' "Rocket to the Moon" premieres in NYC
John Steinbeck's novella "The Pearl" published
Frank Loesser's musical "Guys & Dolls", starring Robert Alda, Vivian Blaine, and Sam Levene, opens at 46th St Theater, NYC; runs for 1200 performances, winning 5 Tony Awards
Brooklyn Dodgers signWalter Alstonto a 1-year pact as manager for 1954; he signs 23 one-year agreements until his retirement after 1976 season
Americans go 1-2 in the men's 100m final at the Melbourne Olympics;Bobby Morrowand Thane Baker both record hand-timed 10.50s but automatic timing gives gold medal to Morrow
Cleveland Browns' fullbackJim Brownsets club record of 237 yds rushing
Philadelphia centerWilt Chamberlainpulls down NBA record 55 rebounds in Warriors 132-129 defeat to Boston Celtics at Philadelphia Civic Center
MarshalMobutu Sese SekoKuku Ngbendu becomes President of Zaire (President until 1997)
The cult classic "One Million Years B.C." starringRaquel Welch, is released 1st in West Germany
TaoiseachJack Lynchmeets with British PMEdward Heathin London to give Irish approval to Attlee's paper saying new arrangements should be 'acceptable to and accepted by the Republic of Ireland'
Gerald FordandLeonid Brezhnevagree to a framework for the SALT-II treaty to reduce each side's number of nuclear weapons, at the Vladivostok Summit
RedsJoe Morganwins his 2nd straight NL MVP Award
"Salem's Lot", American two-part miniseries based on the horror novel of the same name byStephen King, concludes in the United States
OriolesCal Ripkenis named AL Rookie of Year
"The Colour of Magic" byTerry Pratchettpublished by Colin Smythe in the UK, 1st book in the Discworld series
Li Peng succeedsZhao Ziyangas Premier of the People's Republic of China
Sachin Tendulkarscores a Test cricket fifty at the record young age of 16 years and 214 days
Monica Selessets female tennis record, winning prize money of $2,457,758 in a year
AmericanPete Sampraswins his 3rd ATP Tour World Championship tennis titles with a classic 3–6, 7–6, 7–6, 6–7, 6–4 victory over defending championBoris Beckerin Hanover, Germany
German tennis starSteffi Grafretains her WTA Tour Championship with a 6–3, 4–6, 6–0, 4–6, 6–0 win overMartina Hingisat Madison Square Garden, NYC

Leader of the Opposition in the Canadian ParliamentStephen Harper, introduces a motion of no confidence. The motion is passed on November 28 leading to the dissolution of the 38th Canadian Parliament.

Australians elect the Kevin Rudd-led centre-left Australian Labor Party at the federal election, ending the eleven-year tenure of theJohn Howard-led centre-right Liberal/National coalition government.
English singer-rapper-songwriterCraig Davidreleases his first Greatest Hits album
German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelwins season-ending Brazilian Grand Prix; his record 9th straight GP victory; record equalling 13th consecutive GP win; 4th straight World Drivers Championship
Former New York MayorMichael Bloombergannounces a late-bid to be the US Democratic presidential candidate
Dow Jones Industrial Average closes above 30,000 points for the first time, after formal transition toJoe Bidenadministration begins

First observation of the transit of Venus by Jeremiah Horrocks and William Crabtree, helps establish the size of the solar system. The English astronomers predict its course usingJohannes Kepler's method.
The Blitz: Luftwaffe bombs Bristol city centre, killing 200 people in the first German raid on the city
The Beatles began recording sessions for their album "Sgt.Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band"
Senate report proves US troops in Vietnam were exposed to the toxic chemical defoliant Agent Orange
Former First Vice PresidentEmmerson Mnangagwais sworn in as President of Zimbabwe, replacing Robert Mugabe's 30-year tenure
Alphonso, Earl of Chester, son of Edward I of England, born in Bayonne, Gascony (d. 1284)
Charles, Duke of Orléans, French poet and noble who spent 25 years as a prisoner of war, born in Paris (d. 1465)
